description.value:
Trigger A living creature ends their turn in the room

Effect The soul's eyes open, and it cries out in agony as its ghostly body twists and transforms into something monstrous. The triggering PC must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:34|options:inflicts:frightened,inflicts:fleeing] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fleeing] for as long as they're frightened on a critical failure). The haunt then rolls initiative.

stealth.details: (master) to notice that the eerie manifestation is dangerous; noticing the ghostly image of the Arudora in question floating in the middle of the room has a DC of 0

details.description: A ghostly image of one of the Arudoras floats in the middle of the room, arms folded over their chest as if reclining in a coffin, yet they hover vertically and are shrouded by ribbons of green smoke.
details.disable:
@Check[religion|dc:41] to exorcise the haunt, or one of the following checks depending on the Arudora; four total successes are required to disable the haunt.

J3a (Jinnivere) @Check[nature|dc:38] to draw upon her love of horses

J3b (Dazerien) @Check[intimidation|dc:38] to frighten the cruel murderer

J3c (Ryhasphinea) @Check[deception|dc:38] to trick her into believing she's been forsaken by Urgathoa

J3d (Augsten) @Check[diplomacy|dc:38] to explain that abuse of the servants shows weakness of character

J3e (Alisendra) @Check[performance|dc:38] to give her one last entertainment

J3f (Raudltz) @Check[society|dc:38] to help him come to terms with the shame he feels for his infidelities

J3g (Irrokcis) @Check[occultism|dc:38] to use his own obscure theories against him to banish him

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure Path: Bastion of Blasphemies

details.reset: The haunt deactivates as soon as no living creatures are in the room, then immediately resets.
details.routine:
(1 action) On its initiative, the haunt transforms into a physical manifestation of that Arudora's personality and lashes out, dealing @Damage[9d6[untyped]] damage to all creatures in the room and within 30 feet of any open doorway outside the room. A PC can attempt a DC 34 basic saving throw against the damage. The type of damage and saving throw to defend against it depend on the Arudora, as does the additional condition imparted on a critical failure. Conditions imparted by this haunt are curses that have unlimited durations.

J3a (Jinnivere) trampling horses (bludgeoning damage, Reflex save, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Clumsy]{Clumsy 1} on a critical failure)

J3b (Dazerien) complex torture devices (piercing damage, Reflex save, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} on a critical failure)

J3c (Ryhasphinea) skull-faced spirits (spirit damage, Will save, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} on a critical failure)

J3d (Augsten) barbed whip and scourge Strikes (slashing damage, Reflex save, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Enfeebled]{Enfeebled 1} on a critical failure)

J3e (Alisendra) shrieking spirits and discordant instruments (sonic damage, Fortitude save, stupefied 1 on a critical failure)

J3f (Raudltz) ghostly fangs that chew into your thoughts (mental damage, Will save, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Drained]{Drained 1} on a critical failure)

J3g (Irrokcis) decaying skeletal limbs (void damage, Fortitude save, drained 1 on a critical failure)
